pages.raw_text:
BOISE, Dec. 11.-For the second
time Harry Orchard has begun a
recital of his crime-stained life to
jury in the Boise courtroom. 1t
was the same story he had told in
the Haywood trial with a single
deviation. Except for seeming
willingness to tell all without re-
serve. his attitude on the stand is
similar to that of the first trial.


The courtroom was crowded with
women today. Orchard entered
with a brisk step and held his hand
high as he took the oath.


In passing he glanced casually at
Meyer and Pettibone, but was well
along in his testimony when his
eye met theirs. His face did not
show an expression, but his eyes
dropped and he changed position.


Today is the first time Orchard
had seen either men since he left
them in Colorado. Orchard told of
his early life in Colorado, his in
fatuation for the wife of another
man, their elopement, his forsak-
ing the woman and child and COM
ing to Idaho. His description of
the blowing up of the Bunker Hill
& Sullivan mines was precise. He
admitted that he touched off the
fuse. From this act he went into
the Colorado troubles and of his
meeting with Haywood and Petit
bone.


The noon adjournment was taken
with Orchard still on the stand.
